zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilbert III Pecche 1st Lord Peche於1268年出生在英國素福爾可格亞特 比阿林斯時，他的父親Gilbert de Pecche48歲，母親Joan de Creye26歲。他於1300年與Iseult在英國素福爾可格亞特 比阿林斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1322年6月26日在他的家鄉享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Pecche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a variant of   Pech   ‘pitch, tar’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Peče or of its Germanized form Petsche , which is also a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname.

variants.name: Pech
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Manuel, Alvaro, Diego, Edelmira, Emilio, Francisco, Gilberto, Jorge, Jose Angel.  German Gerhard, Hans, Ilse, Lutz, Manfred, Wolf.

Czech and Slovak; Sorbian (Pěch and P'ech): from a pet form of the personal name Petr (Czech), Pětš (Lower Sorbian), and Peter (Slovak). This is also a Germanized form of the Upper Sorbian cognate Pjech.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German pech (German Pech) ‘pitch, tar’, presumably a nickname for someone with exceptionally dark skin or hair, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who prepared, sold, or used pitch. This may in part also be the origin of a homonymic Polish, Czech, and Slovak surname (compare above and below).

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Gilbert III Pecche 1st Lord Peche was born in 1268, in Great Bealings, Suffolk, England, his father, Gilbert de Pecche, was 48 and his mother, Joan de Creye, was 26. He married Iseult in 1300, in Great Bealings, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 26 June 1322, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gilbert III Pecche 1st Lord Peche родился в 1268 г. в местности грит билингс, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Gilbert de Pecche было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Creye, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Iseult в 1300 г. в местности грит билингс, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 26 июня 1322 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.
